task.title: Add config migration command when profile schema changes.

context.problem_statement: Installed repos need an explicit, plan-first way to detect and migrate repo-contract-kit install/profile configuration metadata when the profile or install schema changes, without manually diffing receipts, manifests, or managed-file state.
background: AGW-021 is an older P2 bootstrap backlog row whose original delivery shape was an upgrade subcommand.
The kit already has plan-first update behavior in scripts/update.py and wrapper CLI update-plan/update.
AGW-077 made update.py plan-only by default and preserved mutation behind explicit --apply.
Current install/update metadata records schema_version, profiles, preset, runtime_adapters, source refs, prompt snapshots, managed files, and Makefile boundary state.
The kit checkout is clean at 2b3ef0b (repo-contract-kit 0.4.48), and make version-check && make docs-check passed at packet creation.
non_goals: Do not rewrite the install/update architecture or collapse update-plan, update, kit-update, or kit-refresh into one command.
Do not migrate arbitrary target-owned files or user customizations.
Do not make update planning mutate target repos.
Do not introduce hosted CI, GitHub-only, or PR-bot behavior.
Do not edit agent-workflow-kit prompt/schema source except for source backlog closeout after kit validation passes.

report_sources: git ls-remote origin refs/heads/main for source and kit remotes
finalizer_receipt_paths: source main commit b1c4680fc2f92279f948d820f8e1965cfc7c662e closed AGW-099; source agent-task-status reported no active, stale, hazardous, or unknown-scope tasks
previous_task_state.notes: AGW-021 packet creation began only after AGW-099 was pushed in both repos, both remotes matched local HEADs, both worktrees were clean, and source task-status reported zero active tasks.

closeout_required_before_start.reason: The previous AGW-099 task has source and kit closeout commits, matching remote refs, clean worktrees, and no active task metadata.
closeout_required_before_start.required_next_step: Proceed with exactly one AGW-021 worker in repo-contract-kit; refresh task-status if source or kit state becomes dirty before worker launch.

goal_alignment.repo_goal: Keep repo-contract-kit as the install-layer guardrail provider that can safely install, inspect, plan, and explicitly apply target-repo contract updates without hiding local customizations.

area_contracts.purpose: Plan and apply safe updates for installed repo-contract-kit files, manifests, receipts, profile selection, runtime adapters, and managed-file conflicts.
Defines install profiles, presets, runtime adapters, target-owned files, and install receipt/manifest metadata.
Stable wrapper CLI for update-plan/update and any operator-facing migration command.
Regression coverage for update planning, legacy adoption, managed-file preservation, and apply behavior.
Document operator-facing install/update/migration commands and plan/apply safety boundaries.

stop_conditions: Stop if the design would mutate target repos from a plan command.
Stop if migration requires overwriting target-owned files or customized managed files without an explicit apply/force flag.
Stop if profile-schema drift cannot be represented deterministically in install/manifest metadata and tests.

protected_files: Target-owned files in any installed third-party repo.
Existing plan-first update safety: update-plan must remain non-mutating.
Existing managed-file conflict preservation unless an explicit force/apply flag is used.
expected_outputs: A deterministic current profile/config schema marker in install and manifest metadata, or an equivalent explicit migration-version field.
update-plan JSON/text reports whether installed config/profile metadata is current, missing, outdated, or blocked, with concrete next commands.
An explicit apply path migrates only kit metadata needed for profile-schema/config drift while preserving target-owned files and managed-file conflicts.
Wrapper CLI and installed Make/docs surfaces expose the new migration/update path consistently.
Tests cover current install, missing schema metadata, outdated schema metadata, invalid metadata, no-install, dry-run/no-write, and explicit apply behavior.
VERSION and CHANGELOG.md record the kit behavior change.

acceptance_criteria.description: Install receipts and manifests carry a deterministic profile/config schema version marker or equivalent migration version for future drift detection.
acceptance_criteria.verification: Install/update tests assert the marker appears in new metadata.

acceptance_criteria.description: update-plan detects missing or outdated profile/config schema metadata and reports a migration action without writing target repo files.
acceptance_criteria.verification: Focused update-plan JSON tests assert target_repo_writes.performed is false and expected migration actions/blockers are present.

acceptance_criteria.description: The explicit apply path updates only migration metadata and safe kit-managed metadata needed for schema drift while preserving target-owned files and customized managed-file conflicts.
acceptance_criteria.verification: Focused update apply tests with customized files and old metadata.

acceptance_criteria.description: Wrapper CLI and installed operator docs expose the migration path and keep update-plan non-mutating.
The feature is versioned and documented as a repo-contract-kit behavior change.
acceptance_criteria.verification: VERSION, CHANGELOG.md, docs review, and make version-check.

acceptance_criteria.description: Source backlog rows and summary close AGW-021 only after kit validation passes.

commands.command: PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1 python3 -m unittest tests.test_update tests.test_repo_contract_kit_cli tests.test_install

commands.expected_result: pass in source repo after closeout
pass in source repo after closeout
pass in source repo after closeout

evidence_to_capture: No-write update-plan proof for old schema metadata

readiness_check.expected_result: pass before lifecycle finish, or blocked with explicit receipt if validation cannot complete

lifecycle_action.expected_result: done metadata or explicit blocked finalizer receipt

final_task_status.expected_result: no active AGW-021 task metadata after parent closeout, or terminal metadata with linked receipt before cleanup

closeout_preview.expected_result: preview reports no unsafe dirty terminal worktree removal

closeout_requirements.dirty_state_explanation: Worker must explain any remaining dirty files in the kit checkout; parent should only integrate expected AGW-021 files and leave both repos clean after commits.

docs_impact.notes: This changes install/update operator behavior and metadata contracts, so docs and changelog/version updates are required.

known_risks: Migration code can accidentally overwrite target-owned or customized managed files.
Schema-version naming can confuse install schema, profile selection, and managed-file manifest schema if not documented clearly.
A plan/apply regression could make update-plan mutate target repos.
stop_conditions: Any non-apply command writes target repo files.
Any apply path overwrites target-owned files or conflicts without explicit force semantics.
Tests cannot model old/missing schema metadata deterministically.

approval.notes: User requested implementing all remaining open backlog items with one task agent per item and a committed packet before each task.

handoff.next_packet_hint: After AGW-021, make agent-next should select the next remaining P2 backlog item.
